query: HarnessBridge complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: [Bug]: native codex harness uses broken isolated CODEX_HOME (harness-auth) while ~/.codex works · Issue #70511 · openclaw/openclaw · GitHub
results.content:
`codex/gpt-5.4`
`embeddedHarness.runtime: "codex"`
`CODEX_HOME`

`~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/harness-auth/codex/<hash>`

`~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/harness-auth/codex/<hash>`

On my machine, that generated home is the failing layer.

`~/.codex`
`harness-auth`
`codex/`
`401 Unauthorized: Missing bearer or basic authentication in header`

A local workaround is to override `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command` with a wrapper that forces Codex app-server to use the real working `~/.codex` instead of the generated bridge home.

`plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command`
`~/.codex`

## Environment

`2026.4.21 (f788c88)`
`0.123.0`
`codex/gpt-5.4`

## Why this appears to be a harness-auth bridge bug

The official docs for the Codex harness say:

Docs: [...] The official docs for the Codex harness say:

Docs:

However, the installed OpenClaw Codex harness appears to generate and inject its own isolated Codex home instead of using the already-working local one.

From the installed bundle on my machine:

`dist/extensions/codex/harness-.js`
`prepareCodexAuthBridge({ bridgeDir: "harness-auth", ... })`
`env.CODEX_HOME = bridge.codexHome`

I can reproduce that the generated home is broken while the real home is healthy.

## Reproduction

Result:

`Logged in using ChatGPT`

Example generated dirs on my machine:

`/Users/<user>/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/harness-auth/codex/06bfb5171eff0241
/Users/<user>/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/harness-auth/codex/a9df8fee07a27f2d`

Actual result: [...] Configured as:

This is not ideal, because it bypasses the harness-auth bridge entirely and relies on the user discovering and wiring a wrapper manually.

## Expected behavior

One of these should be true:

`harness-auth`
`~/.codex`

## Important distinction

This is not just “my OAuth token is bad.”

On the same machine:

`~/.codex`
`harness-auth`

So the breakage appears to be in the OpenClaw Codex auth-home bridge layer, not in the underlying local Codex login.

 metrics, [...] Introducing 'HarnessBridge', an agent-environmental interface module, to solve the problem of duplicate information accumulation and inefficient interaction that occurs during long-term work. HarnessBridge optimizes the flow of information using learnable bidirectional projection technology between large-scale language model (LLM) agents and environments. Specifically, observation projection reduces token usage by compressing the massive execution history into a core state, and action projection increases work efficiency by blocking unfounded suggestions and providing feedback. results.title: [Literature Review] HarnessBridge: Learnable Bidirectional Controller for LLM Agent Harness
results.content: The paper evaluates HarnessBridge on challenging long-horizon benchmarks, Terminal-Bench 2.0 and SWE-bench Verified, using a range of open-source (e.g., Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, GLM4.7-Flash) and commercial (e.g., GPT-5.4-nano, Claude-Opus-4.7) generator models. HarnessBridge, initialized from a lightweight Qwen3.5-0.8B model, demonstrates superior or competitive performance compared to strong specialized and auto-harness baselines. It significantly reduces average input token usage (up to -77.5%) and trajectory length, while matching or improving success rates (e.g., +11.2 percentage points on Terminal-Bench 2.0 with Qwen3.5-35B-A3B). [...] If $d\_t = \text{Pass}$, then $\mathcal{a}'\_t = \mathcal{a}\_t$, and the action is executed. If $d\_t = \text{Reject}$, then $\mathcal{a}'\_t = \emptyset$, and the proposed action is blocked. In case of rejection, $\rho\_t$ is returned to the generator, comprising a (concern, evidence, suggestion) tuple, where the evidence must be grounded in the current interaction history. This prevents the agent from performing redundant, ungrounded, or unproductive actions, thereby conserving environment steps and directing the agent towards more effective paths. [...] Analysis of trajectory case studies and category-level behavior reveals that observation projection is highly selective, compressing verbose but less critical categories like "reasoning\_only" while preserving crucial ones like "test\_execution," and distilling key facts into the active-state index. Outcome-level efficiency analysis shows that HarnessBridge reduces both interaction turns and token consumption across all task outcome categories (pass, fail, gained, lost), with larger token reductions per turn, indicating more compact context. Notably, for tasks where HarnessBridge succeeded and baselines failed ("Gained" category), it reduced turns by 65% and token usage by 89%, suggesting that its learned control patterns enable earlier convergence by maintaining a more concise and

results.title: Harness Support FAQs | Answers to Common Support Questions
results.content:
# Support FAQs

## Effective support request

Want to know what is the best way to report an issue, or ask a question? Follow these guidelines.

### Issues, problems, or bugs

When reporting an issue, it is best to include the following components:

### Feature requests

If reporting a request for new functionality, make sure to include:

### Questions

If asking a question, please tell us:

## What happens when a ticket is created?

### Ticket Routing Flow

## CSAT survey [...] You can do this yourself by logging into the support portal and marking the ticket as solved.

Please note that only the ticket's main contact (the requester) may close the ticket. You can not solve tickets you are copied on.

## Copying others on support tickets

You may add others to your support ticket as CC. This means that they will be copied on all messages to/from Harness Support and yourself.

If you are using email, just copy the new person on the email message, and they will be copied on all future communication.

If you are using the support portal, you can mark others as copied. You can also ask your support engineer to add others as copied.

‍

## Access to Support Portal

Harness support portal for our end users is accessible via following URL Harness Support Portal. [...] ## Automatic close of pending issues

A support ticket is considered pending if it is waiting for a response from the ticket originator (the requester). Harness will send an automatic reminder two and four business days after the ticket is set to Pending. After five business days, if no response has been received, a final message will be sent, and the ticket will be marked solved, regardless of status.

The requester may re-open a solved ticket within two weeks. After that time, a ticket is marked closed and cannot be reopened.

## Code of conduct

Harness is committed to treating all customers, prospects, partners, and users with respect, empathy, and kindness, as outlined in the Harness Support Code Of Conduct.

results.title: Get support
results.content:
If you prefer email, or if you can't reach the dashboard support panel, you can open new a normal-severity ticket for your default team via email to bridge-support[at]crunchydata.com. Send the email with the description of your issue from the address associated with your Crunchy Bridge account.

## Corresponding on tickets

Notifications about your support tickets will be sent via email from bridge-support[at]crunchydata.com. Make sure to add this address to your email allow list. You can correspond with the Crunchy Bridge CSE Team by replying to these emails, or you can respond on tickets through the dashboard interface. [...] ## Accessing the support page

You can access support in the Crunchy Bridge dashboard by navigating to your Team page and clicking on the "Support" tab:

The default list shows open tickets for the team. You can also review closed tickets and drafts you've started by clicking on the tabs in the support panel. You can click here to jump to your Team's support page.

Info

Only team administrators and team managers can create or view support tickets for their team. If you do not see the "Support" tab for your Team or get a 404 on a support link, ask a Team administrator or manager for assistance.

## Opening a ticket

To open a new ticket, click the + New Ticket button in the support panel shown above. [...] | Ticket severity | Guidelines |
 --- |
| Low |  General questions that are not time-sensitive  Billing, account, and other non-technical queries |
| Normal |  Minor or low-impact production issues  Most issues related to staging or development environments |
| High |  Production cluster is up and available but experiencing degraded performance, and  The degradation in performance is affecting upstream applications, and  There may be a workaround available, but the issue is having an impact on the business  Your staging cluster is inoperable or you're not able to connect to it |
| Urgent |  Your production cluster is inoperable or you're not able to connect to it, and  The issue is having a critical impact on the business, and  There is no workaround available |

Info
